summary: Windows packaged desktop CUDA startup failed before Registered: yes because a stale system site-packages pathlib.py backport shadowed Python 3.11 stdlib pathlib inside the llama_cpp subprocess facade.
impact: Packaged Windows compute nodes could warm-load CUDA far enough to select a compute plan but fail before relay registration, preventing staging round-robin validation; the same import-order seam also affected macOS packaged runtime paths.
fix: Kept stdlib paths ahead of site-packages when packaged bootstrap, runtime probes, and llama_cpp facade children mutate sys.path; added stdlib shadowing diagnostics; stopped passing ambient PYTHONPATH into runtime probes; and extended real-relay packaged e2e coverage with polluted site-packages fixtures for standard and macOS Contents/Resources layouts.
lessons: PYTHONNOUSERSITE is not enough for packaged desktop runtime isolation because system site-packages can contain stale stdlib backports. E2E coverage must launch the real relay and fail unless the packaged runtime-backed bridge reaches registered=true, not merely Running: yes.
